{{Quote|Man, droid and monarch alike is but a composite of technicalities. Enshrined by his promises.|[[Sarsius Torne]], quoting Plaristes|<ref name="SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref>}}
'''Plaristes''' was a [[Philosopher/Legends|philosopher]] who lived in the [[Time/Legends|time]] before the formation of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[25,053 BBY|over 25,000 years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. In his [[Book/Legends|book]] ''[[Of Minds, Men, and Machines]]'', he argued that [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were incapable of attaining artificial [[Sentience/Legends|sentience]]. In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following, however, the development of increasingly sophisticated models of droids would prove him wrong, although his beliefs on the fundamental limitations of droid sentience would nonetheless remain widespread throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].
